'''Martin Wood''' (born 24 June 1970) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s who played as a [[Five-eighth|stand-off]] or [[Rugby league positions#Lock|loose forward]] for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Scarborough Pirates]], [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]] and [[Workington Town]].

==Career==
Wood started his career at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], helping the club achieve promotion from the [[Rugby Football League Championship Second Division|Second Division]] in 1990–91. He was deemed surplus to requirements during the following season, and was sold to [[Scarborough Pirates]] in October 1991.<ref>{{cite web|title=Sale of Martin was controversial|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/opinion/columnists/sale-of-martin-was-controversial-1-1905807|website=Halifax Courier|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=6 February 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304060007/http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/opinion/columnists/sale-of-martin-was-controversial-1-1905807|archive-date=4 March 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref> Wood then joined [[Keighley Cougars]] in 1992.<ref name="halloffame">{{cite web|title=Cougars legend enters Hall of Fame|url=http://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/sportrugbyleague/rugbyleaguecougars/9037560.Cougars_legend_enters_Hall_of_Fame/|website=Keighley News|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=19 May 2011}}</ref>

In January 1997, Wood and his team-mate [[Nick Pinkney]] were signed by [[Super League]] side Sheffield Eagles in exchange for £95,000 plus [[Mark Gamson]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Rugby League: Larder looks to former club|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-larder-looks-to-former-club-1284383.html|website=The Independent|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=21 January 1997}}</ref> He spent two seasons at Sheffield. Wood appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.

He returned to Keighley in 1999.<ref name="halloffame" /> During his second spell at the club, he was named the [[Northern Ford Premiership]] Player of the Year in 2000,<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Wood wins new award but age bars way to Halifax|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/wood-winsnew-award-but-age-bars-way-to-halifax-708776.html|website=The Independent|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=26 July 2000}}</ref> and reached a career total of 1,000 points in January 2001.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Fisher|first1=Michael|title=Keighley go three points clear at top|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/4776547/Keighley-go-three-points-clear-at-top.html|website=Telegraph|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=31 January 2001}}</ref> He finished his professional career with [[Workington Town]] in 2002.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilson|first1=Andy|title=Hull ride luck to hold out|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2002/feb/18/rugbyleague.hullkr|website=The Guardian|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=18 February 2002}}</ref> He continued playing and coaching rugby at amateur level, helping Sharlston Rovers achieve a shock win against [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the third round of the [[2004 Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Rovers produce magic to fell Rams|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/all-clubs/rovers-produce-magic-to-fell-rams-1-2539207|website=Yorkshire Post|accessdate=29 September 2014|date=6 February 2004}}</ref>

In 2011, Wood was inducted into Keighley's Hall of Fame.<ref name="halloffame" />
